methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 53.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Argotec is a growth-stage space and aerospace company founded by David Avino, currently targeting €75M in funding. The valuation landscape encompasses 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace.

Standard triangulation methods yield limited actionable results. Peer-median analysis is not applicable: while 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, the narrow filters (same stage, adjacent stage, similar valuation tier) produced fewer than 3 datapoints, making a median unreliable. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied without Argotec's disclosed revenue; the public Space & Aerospace sector trades at a 53.2x price-to-sales median across the 6 comparable tickers, but this multiple is only meaningful if the target's top-line revenue is known.

The absence of sufficient Stage-matched private peers and missing revenue data prevents quantitative triangulation at this time. Any valuation conclusion would require either: (1) Argotec's disclosed or estimated revenue to anchor the 53.2x public P/S multiple, or (2) expansion of the peer set to adjacent stages with documented financing multiples. The €75M fundraising target itself does not constitute a valuation floor without context on equity percentage sold. Further financial disclosure or comparable transaction data in the Space & Aerospace growth segment is necessary to support a defensible range.
